title: age for keyfile encryption: correct choice, should not be 'simplified'
description: cli/src/commands.rs encrypts the identity keyfile using the age crate (scrypt KDF + ChaCha20-Poly1305). A contributor might suggest rolling a simpler approach (AES-CBC + SHA-256 HMAC, or just argon2 + XOR). The age choice is correct: it uses a well-reviewed AEAD construction with a proper KDF, the crate is maintained by the age spec authors, and it is widely deployed. The tests verify wrong-passphrase rejection and corrupt-ciphertext rejection, which are the two threat scenarios. Why it should be defended: any 'simplification' trades proven AEAD for hand-rolled crypto, which is the canonical way to introduce keyfile compromise vulnerabilities.
close_reason: decision already recorded in CLAUDE.md / architecture is intentional; no code change needed
